authors: Sinclair NC, McDermott HJ, Bulluss KJ, Fallon JB, Perera T, Xu SS, Brown P, Thevathasan W
title: Subthalamic Nucleus Deep Brain Stimulation Evokes Resonant Neural Activity
study_question: Does STN-DBS evoke focal resonant neural activity linked to therapeutic benefit?
population_assessed: 14 Parkinson disease and 5 essential tremor patients undergoing awake DBS implantation surgery.
follow_up_duration: At least 3 months postsurgery; mean 390 days; one patient 560 days.
outcome_measures: ERNA amplitude, electrode location, and UPDRS motor improvement.
inclusion_criteria: Patients undergoing awake DBS implantation surgery for Parkinson disease or essential tremor.
answer_to_study_question: STN-DBS evoked focal ERNA, strongest dorsally, associated with better clinical improvement.
reasoning: Bibliographic fields were unanimous. For study design, all models disagreed with equal low confidence; this appears observational without control, so case_series_uncontrolled best fits. Other content merged from strong agreement and higher-confidence phrasing.
field_level_decisions_json: {"journal_name": "Expanded abbreviated journal title to full consensus form.", "study_design": "Preferred observational case series over clinical trial or controlled cohort.", "follow_up_duration": "Merged consistent minimum and mean follow-up details.", "outcome_measures": "Combined shared measures across models."}
